If Alejandro is telling the truth, then his statement is true. But if he's lying, then his statement is false, meaning Kandi doesn't tell the truth. But we already know Kandi does tell the truth, so maybe Alejandro is telling the truth? Wait, but how do I know if Alejandro is truthful or not? Let me hold that thought. Next, Millicent says Alejandro lies. So Millicent is claiming that Alejandro is lying. If Millicent is telling the truth, then Alejandro is lying, which would mean his statement that "Kandi tells the truth" is false. If Millicent says Alejandro lies, then if Millicent is truthful, Alejandro is lying. But Alejandro said Kandi tells the truth, which is a true statement. Therefore, if Alejandro is lying, then his statement would be false, implying Kandi doesn't tell the truth, which contradicts the given information.
